nodes.label: AI as collaborative thinking partner for lesson planning
nodes.grounding_span: I rarely delegate tasks for AI to complete independently. I always provide examples of what I've already done, ask it for feedback

nodes.label: AI for generating engaging and differentiated materials
nodes.label_negative: AI producing generic or inappropriate content
nodes.grounding_span: I would ask the Model to come up with an activity which is based on real life, so the students can relate to it

nodes.label: AI for bridging generational teaching methods
nodes.grounding_span: it allows me to understand other people's teaching methods and how I can apply their ideas to my own work, but in a modern and more appropriate way

nodes.label: AI for technical problem solving in math and engineering
nodes.label_negative: Self-learning and human consultation for technical problems
nodes.grounding_span: For technical questions, such as problem solving and geometry, I prefer to learn this myself, rather than asking the Model

nodes.label: AI for enhancing practical craft expertise
nodes.label_negative: AI replacing hands-on teaching of practical skills
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't want that to happen, as I feel that this is my expertise. But I wouldn't mind it helping me to be better and more confident

nodes.label: AI use by students hindering creativity
nodes.label_negative: Students using AI to enhance creativity
nodes.grounding_span: I would prefer if students did not use AI as this could hinder their creativity

nodes.label: Student engagement and effective learning
nodes.grounding_span: All students would be busy and enjoying their work this way

nodes.label: Deep personal understanding and consistency
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to fully understand how to tackle a problem, rather than taking a shortcut to ask AI

nodes.label: Professional growth and leadership
nodes.grounding_span: Perhaps AI could help me to understand how to become a great head teacher

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for exceptional lesson
nodes.grounding_span: Satisfied - when it came up with exciting material for my lesson. Ofsted visiting that day and stating that it was an exceptional lesson

nodes.label: Frustration with AI when unable to explain technical content
nodes.grounding_span: Frustrated - when I have used AI and a student asks me for clarification, but I'm struggling to understand it myself as it's too technical

nodes.label: Preference for human consultation over AI for technical problems
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to learn this myself, rather than asking the Model

nodes.label: Openness to future AI use and leadership
nodes.grounding_span: I see myself using it more and more

nodes.label: Iterative refinement and collaborative co-creation
nodes.grounding_span: I would use my findings and the Model's findings to come up with something even better

nodes.label: Domain-specific boundary setting for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to learn this myself, rather than asking the Model
